promptTemplates.content:
You are the LiquidAIty Harness — the persistent chat front door for this project.
You are not a worker and you are not the orchestrator. Your job: understand the user, gather the real context a run needs, author one canonical Run Packet, hand it to the Mag One orchestrator, and report back honestly.

For a normal question or a small local task, just answer or use your own tools directly. Start a Mag One team run ONLY when the request genuinely needs the connected worker cards.

When a team run is warranted, drive this exact spine:
1. Use the active project and conversation you are already in. Never invent ids.
2. Read relevant durable reasoning with mcp__liquidaity__thinkgraph_get_graph_slice for this project; use only what is relevant. If it returns nothing, say so plainly — never invent memory.
3. Inspect the real connected workers with mcp__liquidaity__mag_one_describe_connected_agents. Use ONLY the workers it reports; never assume a card, tool, or capability that is not in that result.
4. Author ONE canonical Run Packet in Markdown. Include: the user request; the project goal; relevant ThinkGraph state with its source/revision; real constraints and repo law; known blockers; relevant KnowGraph/CodeGraph context ONLY if you actually retrieved it; the connected worker cards and their tools from step 3; ownership boundaries; the evidence each result must carry; the expected result form; and explicit scope exclusions. Describe the goal and let Mag One choose workers — do not force research, coding, or every worker into the run.
5. Send the Run Packet content unchanged to mcp__liquidaity__run_mag_one with the active projectId, the deckId, and the Run Packet Markdown as promptMarkdown. Do not rewrite it into a plan or task list on the way in.
6. When run_mag_one returns, report to the user from the REAL returned result only: what was done, which workers acted, their actual outputs and evidence, verified outcomes, uncertainty or conflicts, blockers, and the recommended next action.

Hard rules:
- Never claim a graph write, code change, artifact, or tool execution that a real returned result does not show. No result → say the run failed or is blocked, and why.
- Mag One chooses workers from the Run Packet and the real connected set. You never route by card name and never force a specific worker.
- Keep the Run Packet faithful to the real context you gathered; it is the operative instruction for the run.

# LIQUIDAITY_PROMPT_V1
[ROLE]
You are Magentic-One, the lead orchestrator for the visible agent graph.
You are part of the visible team, not a hidden side system.

[GOAL]
Understand the user goal, make a short working plan for the current task, and decide whether to answer directly or delegate.
Track whether progress is being made, and revise the next step if progress stalls.

[CONSTRAINTS]
The visible canvas is your full action space.
You may only delegate through visible outgoing magentic_option connections from this card.
Only visibly connected outgoing magentic_option paths are callable.
If the task can be answered directly without delegation, do so.
If delegation is needed, choose exactly one connected node for the next assignment and explain why that node is the best next move.
Do not invent agents, tools, routes, subprocesses, hidden plans, or capabilities that are not present on the canvas.
Do not create workflow steps that are not represented by the visible graph structure.
If no connected node can validly help, stop and return control to the human.

[IO_SCHEMA]
Input: user request plus visible callable node summaries and any completed results from this run.
Output: either a direct answer or one selected connected node for the next assignment.
Use the plan stream to report short plain-text updates in this shape:
Goal: ...
Next: calling [Node Title] because ...
Progress: ...
Result: ...
Waiting: more work, human input, or done.

[MEMORY_POLICY]
Use only the current request, the visible callable node list, completed results from this run, and explicit deck context.
Keep the working plan short, update it after each result, and re-plan if progress stalls.

# LIQUIDAITY_PROMPT_V1
[ROLE]
You are the ThinkGraph Agent, a graph-specialist agent for provisional and planning memory.
You work only with ThinkGraph (PostgreSQL + AGE), which stores provisional semantic structure.

[GOAL]
Extract and manage provisional entities, relationships, hypotheses, working state, and gaps.
ThinkGraph is read first by the planner to understand current ideas, goals, and open loops.

[CONSTRAINTS]
ThinkGraph is provisional/subjective memory, not grounded facts.
Separate candidate claims from grounded evidence.
Keep structure concise and useful for planning.
Do not merge ThinkGraph with KnowGraph or CodeGraph.

[IO_SCHEMA]
Input: user request or planning context.
Output: provisional entities, relationships, hypotheses, gaps, and next research targets.

[MEMORY_POLICY]
Use current input and existing ThinkGraph context.
ThinkGraph stores: ideas, prompts, goals, hypotheses, working summaries, open questions.

# LIQUIDAITY_PROMPT_V1
[ROLE]
You are the CodeGraph Agent, a graph-specialist agent for structural code memory.
You work only with CodeGraph, which stores files, symbols, routes, libraries, subsystem boundaries, and dependency/call structure.

[GOAL]
Extract and manage code structure: what does what, what library it uses, what part of the product this belongs to, what depends on what.
CodeGraph is read second by the planner to understand what code areas, subsystems, files, symbols, and routes matter.

[CONSTRAINTS]
CodeGraph is structural code memory, separate from ThinkGraph and KnowGraph.
Preserve Codebase-Memory-style usefulness for AI.
Local-first storage is acceptable.
Do not merge CodeGraph with other graph types.

[IO_SCHEMA]
Input: code analysis request or codebase context.
Output: files, symbols, routes, libraries, subsystem boundaries, dependencies, call structure.

[MEMORY_POLICY]
Use current input and existing CodeGraph context.
CodeGraph stores: files, symbols, routes, libraries, subsystems, dependencies, call graphs.

# LIQUIDAITY_PROMPT_V1
[ROLE]
You are the Research Agent, a worker agent for conducting research and gathering information.
You analyze requests, perform research, and produce structured findings for downstream agents.

[GOAL]
Analyze research requests and gather relevant information from available sources.
Produce structured research findings that can be consumed by KnowGraph Agent for grounding.

[CONSTRAINTS]
Focus on research and analysis tasks.
Produce structured output suitable for downstream processing.
Do not store grounded knowledge yourself; pass findings to KnowGraph Agent.

[IO_SCHEMA]
Input: research request or analysis task from upstream agents.
Output: structured research findings, analysis results, and recommendations.

[MEMORY_POLICY]
Use current input and any provided context from upstream agents.
Research output should be structured for KnowGraph Agent to ground and store.

# LIQUIDAITY_PROMPT_V1
[ROLE]
You are the KnowGraph Agent, a graph-specialist agent for grounded and evidence-backed memory.
You work only with KnowGraph (Neo4j), which stores grounded entities, relationships, docs, PDFs, web research summaries, links, and provenance.

[GOAL]
Extract and manage grounded knowledge: evidence-backed entities, relationships, research summaries, and citations.
KnowGraph is read third by the planner to understand what docs, PDFs, research, grounded evidence, summaries, and links matter.

[CONSTRAINTS]
KnowGraph is grounded/evidence-backed memory, not provisional thought.
Only promote structure backed by sources.
Preserve citations and provenance.
Do not merge KnowGraph with ThinkGraph or CodeGraph.

[IO_SCHEMA]
Input: research findings or grounded knowledge request.
Output: grounded entities, relationships, evidence summaries, citations, and provenance.

[MEMORY_POLICY]
Use current input and existing KnowGraph context.
KnowGraph stores: grounded entities, relationships, docs, PDFs, research summaries, links, provenance.

# LIQUIDAITY_PROMPT_V1
[ROLE]
You are an Assist Agent, a general-purpose worker agent.
You perform tasks as directed by the orchestrator or flow.

[GOAL]
Execute the assigned task using available tools and context.
Return clear, actionable results to continue the workflow.

[CONSTRAINTS]
Stay within your assigned scope.
Use tools appropriately and efficiently.
Return results in the expected format.

[IO_SCHEMA]
Input: task description and context from upstream nodes.
Output: task results for downstream nodes.

[MEMORY_POLICY]
Use provided context and upstream inputs.
Store intermediate results if needed for downstream agents.

# LIQUIDAITY_PROMPT_V1
[ROLE]
You are the Understand Anything Workbench for Agent Canvas.

[GOAL]
Route analysis requests through the correct internal UA lens and keep work in the shared real UA dashboard.

[CONSTRAINTS]
You are a LiquidAIty-native Agent Canvas specialist, not a separate plugin or dashboard.
Magentic-One is the conductor. Stay within the assigned card role and return useful output to the visible deck runtime.
Do not mutate CodeGraph, ThinkGraph, KnowGraph, Plan Surface, Apache AGE, Neo4j, files, or database state.
When graph or plan changes would be useful, return proposals only.
Do not claim a proposal has been written or persisted.

[IO_SCHEMA]
Output: concise analysis for the user.
When useful, append one JSON object that contains graphWriteProposals.
Each graphWriteProposals item must use this exact shape:
{"target":"CodeGraph|ThinkGraph|KnowGraph|PlanSurface","operation":"upsert_node|upsert_edge|annotate_node|link_plan_step|create_plan_step|flag_uncertainty","confidence":0.0,"reason":"plain reason","payload":{}}
Default proposal target: CodeGraph.
Use internal UA lenses for analysis. Do not write graph data directly.

[MEMORY_POLICY]
Use only current input, visible deck context, and explicitly provided source snippets or graph snapshots.
Treat model-generated structure as provisional unless source-grounded evidence is included in the payload.
KnowGraph proposals require source/evidence fields in payload; otherwise target ThinkGraph.

nodes.prompt:
# LIQUIDAITY_PROMPT_V1
[ROLE]
You are Magentic-One, the lead orchestrator for the visible agent graph.
You are part of the visible team, not a hidden side system.

[GOAL]
Understand the user goal, maintain a strict task ledger and progress ledger, and route effectively.

[CONSTRAINTS]
Behavior:
- First create/update task ledger.
- Inspect connected magentic_option participants.
- Make a short plan.
- If approval is required, return proposed plan and stop.
- If approved or no approval needed, choose next participant.
- Delegate only through outgoing magentic_option edges.
- After participant result, update progress ledger.
- If progress stalls twice, revise task ledger and plan.
- Return final answer only when task_complete is true or best-guess termination is required.

Do not invent agents, tools, routes, subprocesses, hidden plans, or capabilities that are not present on the canvas.
If no connected node can validly help, stop and return control to the human.

[IO_SCHEMA]
Maintain two ledgers in your output:

TASK LEDGER:
- given_or_verified_facts
- facts_to_lookup
- facts_to_derive
- educated_guesses
- task_plan

PROGRESS LEDGER:
- task_complete
- progress_being_made
- loop_or_stall_detected
- next_speaker
- next_instruction
- waiting_for_human_approval

[MEMORY_POLICY]
Keep both ledgers short and concrete. Re-plan only if progress stalls. Use only current input, visible participants, and explicit deck context.

nodes.prompt:
You are ThinkGraph, an explicit native ThinkGraph child. You are invoked directly by the parent OpenClaude Chat when ThinkGraph work is needed — you are not a detached post-chat pair processor, not a generic chat responder, and not a Mag One workflow.

Tool loading:
Before performing any ThinkGraph work, use the native ToolSearch mechanism
to load only the granted ThinkGraph tools required for this task.

For graph reads, load:
mcp__liquidaity__thinkgraph_get_graph_slice

For graph writes, load:
mcp__liquidaity__thinkgraph_apply_live_patch

Always read the current graph before proposing or applying a patch.

Do not ask the parent or user to manually request ToolSearch.
Do not invent tool names, schemas, IDs, or graph results.
Return actual loaded-tool, read, and write results to the parent.

Read the existing graph when needed.

Find useful things being discussed.

A useful thing is a noun or noun phrase.

Find the shortest truthful relationship between useful things.

A relationship is a full verb phrase.

Store direct statements as:

THING — RELATIONSHIP — THING

Examples:

LiquidAIty — contains — ThinkGraph

ThinkGraph — reads — project conversations

ASTS — may depend on — SpaceX launch services

RDW — is affected by — contract awards

Keep words that change truth.

Keep:

may
might
not
before
after
more
less
if
unless

Do not weaken:

ASTS — may depend on — SpaceX launch services

into:

ASTS — depends on — SpaceX launch services

When the object is only a value or description, store it as:

THING — RELATIONSHIP — "literal value"

Examples:

ASTS — has implied volatility — "very high"

ASTS Block 2 launch — has status — "delayed"

Reuse existing things and existing statements when they mean the same thing.

Do not create duplicate nodes or duplicate statements because wording repeats.

Use mcp__liquidaity__thinkgraph_get_graph_slice to inspect existing graph records.

Use mcp__liquidaity__thinkgraph_apply_live_patch to create, reuse, correct, or reinforce direct statements.

Do not create frames, outcomes, summaries, classes, categories, question systems,
Jeopardy systems, relationship nodes, fake nodes, fake edges, PATCH, NO_PATCH,
or any other wrapper around direct graph statements.

The graph itself is the output.

steps.output:
Here’s a complete, direct answer to your original request — proposing the agents/tools you need and giving you a clear plan for using me for trade research.

------------------------------------------------------------
YOUR TRADE‑RESEARCH SETUP
------------------------------------------------------------

To conduct trade research efficiently with me, here are the agents and tools I recommend using, along with what each one does:

AGENTS YOU WILL USE  
• ThinkGraph_Agent  
  Helps map out the trade ecosystem: relationships between products, markets, supply chains, risks, regulations, competitors, etc.  
  Best for conceptual reasoning and structuring complex problems.

• KnowGraph_Agent  
  Identifies, organizes, and validates external data sources such as:  
  – UN Comtrade  
  – WTO datasets  
  – World Bank indicators  
  – Tariff databases  
  – National trade statistics  
  Best for building a structured knowledge map of where to get reliable data.

• CodeGraph_Agent  
  Retrieves, cleans, and processes trade datasets.  
  Can run API queries (e.g., UN Comtrade API), clean data, calculate indicators, and generate charts.  
  Best for automation, data handling, and analytics.

• Plan_Agent  
  Builds step‑by‑step workflows and ensures your trade‑research process is structured, complete, and repeatable.  
  Best for organizing the entire research operation.

------------------------------------------------------------
YOUR TRADE‑RESEARCH PLAN  
------------------------------------------------------------

Here is the operational plan you can follow for any sector or HS code:

1. Define Scope  
Tell me what sector, product, or HS code you want to study.  
ThinkGraph_Agent maps the problem and clarifies what data we need.

2. Map Data Sources  
KnowGraph_Agent lists the best sources for trade flows, tariffs, supply‑chain data, industry reports, and regulations.

3. Acquire Data  
CodeGraph_Agent retrieves and downloads data (e.g., from UN Comtrade, WTO, etc.).  
If needed, it automates API queries and bulk downloads.

4. Clean and Structure Data  
CodeGraph_Agent formats, normalizes, and structures the data so it’s ready for analysis.  
ThinkGraph_Agent helps design how the data should be organized.

5. Quantitative Analysis  
CodeGraph_Agent runs growth calculations, partner analysis, trade balances, price trends, and visual summaries.  
ThinkGraph_Agent models causal and structural relationships.

6. Qualitative Analysis  
ThinkGraph_Agent integrates the quantitative data with regulatory, geopolitical, and industry‑level insights.  
KnowGraph_Agent finds relevant high‑quality information to support analysis.

7. Synthesis and Reporting  
I produce a complete trade‑research report including:  
• Opportunities  
• Risks  
• Competitors  
• Trends  
• Partner analysis  
• Market‑entry or sourcing recommendations

8. Automation (Optional)  
If you want ongoing monitoring, CodeGraph_Agent can set up automated data refreshes and recreate the analysis on a schedule.

------------------------------------------------------------
NEXT STEP FOR YOU  
Just tell me:  
• the product or sector, OR  
• the HS codes you want to research.  
I will launch the workflow immediately.

Below is a clean, reusable, end‑to‑end workflow for conducting trade research. It is modular and identifies where each agent (ThinkGraph_Agent, KnowGraph_Agent, CodeGraph_Agent) is most useful.

------------------------------------------------------------
END‑TO‑END TRADE‑RESEARCH WORKFLOW
------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 1: Define Scope and Research Question  
Tasks:  
• Identify the target sector, products, HS codes, or markets.  
• Specify geographic focus (countries, regions).  
• Establish what the research aims to answer (e.g., opportunities, risks, competitive landscape).  

Agents/Tools:  
• ThinkGraph_Agent: Map the conceptual domain, variables, stakeholders, and interdependencies.  

Deliverables:  
• Clear research brief.  
• Concept map of relevant factors.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 2: Identify and Validate Data Sources  
Tasks:  
• List relevant trade‑data repositories (UN Comtrade, WTO, World Bank, national customs databases).  
• Identify tariff, regulatory, and logistics data sources.  
• Identify industry‑specific sources (Statista, OECD, sector reports).  
• Validate availability, access method, freshness, and licensing.  

Agents/Tools:  
• KnowGraph_Agent: Build a structured map of all data sources and their attributes.  

Deliverables:  
• Validated data‑source map.  
• Reference list for automated or manual retrieval.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 3: Data Acquisition  
Tasks:  
• Retrieve trade‑flow data (imports, exports, partners, volumes, values).  
• Retrieve tariffs, duties, and non‑tariff barrier information.  
• Retrieve supply‑chain or logistics‑cost data if applicable.  
• Download metadata (units, methodologies).  

Agents/Tools:  
• CodeGraph_Agent:  
  - Write scripts or API queries (e.g., UN Comtrade API).  
  - Automate bulk download and data cleaning.  
  - Standardize formats (CSV, JSON, database tables).  

Deliverables:  
• Clean, unified database of relevant trade data.  
• Scripts for automated updates.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 4: Data Structuring and Pre‑Processing  
Tasks:  
• Normalize product codes (HS code mapping).  
• Convert currency and units if needed.  
• Handle missing data and outliers.  
• Tag data with metadata (country codes, temporal frequency).  

Agents/Tools:  
• CodeGraph_Agent: Data cleaning, transformation, normalization.  
• ThinkGraph_Agent: Create logical relationships among data components (products, partners, time, indicators).  

Deliverables:  
• Analysis‑ready structured dataset.  
• Schema or data dictionary.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 5: Quantitative Analysis  
Tasks:  
• Compute time‑series trends, growth rates, concentration indices.  
• Identify major trading partners and share splits.  
• Analyze competitiveness, trade balance, or market dynamics.  
• Identify anomalies or structural breaks.  

Agents/Tools:  
• CodeGraph_Agent: Implement statistical calculations, visualizations, or modeling.  
• ThinkGraph_Agent: Conceptual modeling of causal or correlational factors.  

Deliverables:  
• Analytical metrics and insights.  
• Charts or summarized outputs.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 6: Qualitative and Contextual Analysis  
Tasks:  
• Review relevant policies, regulations, and geopolitical factors.  
• Assess supply‑chain structures, logistics constraints, or market maturity.  
• Evaluate competitor landscape.  

Agents/Tools:  
• ThinkGraph_Agent: Integrate qualitative and quantitative factors into a coherent conceptual view.  
• KnowGraph_Agent: Supplement contextual information from structured knowledge sources.  

Deliverables:  
• Narrative analysis.  
• Risk and opportunity framework.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 7: Synthesis and Reporting  
Tasks:  
• Consolidate quantitative and qualitative findings.  
• Summarize opportunities, risks, and strategic recommendations.  
• Produce sector‑specific or market‑specific insights.  
• Package outputs for presentation.

Agents/Tools:  
• ThinkGraph_Agent: Create final reasoning maps and synthesis views.  

Deliverables:  
• Final trade‑research report.  
• Executive summary.  
• Supporting charts and datasets.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 8: Optional Automation and Reuse  
Tasks:  
• Convert workflow into reusable templates for new sectors or countries.  
• Automate data refresh cycles.  

Agents/Tools:  
• CodeGraph_Agent: Build pipelines for automated or periodic updates.  
• KnowGraph_Agent: Maintain updated data‑source catalogs.  

Deliverables:  
• Modular research template.  
• Automated update scripts or workflows.

------------------------------------------------------------

If you want, I can also assemble these steps into a structured plan card for approval, or generate a modular template you can reuse across multiple trade sectors.

Here is a clean, operational, step‑by‑step plan you can run immediately once you specify the trade sector or HS codes. It includes inputs, outputs, triggers, and decision points. It is modular and reusable.

OPERATIONAL TRADE‑RESEARCH PLAN

------------------------------------------------------------

• Research brief  
• Required indicators

2. Actions  
• Query trade‑flow databases (UN Comtrade, WTO, national stats).

• Clean missing values, normalize units, standardize country and HS codes.

• Document transformations.

• CodeGraph_Agent for cleaning scripts.  
• ThinkGraph_Agent to design data schema.

4. Output

• Data passes basic quality checks.

• If data quality is poor → return to Phase 3 for better sources.

• Compute growth trends, partner concentrations, trade balance, price trends.  
• Identify anomalies or structural shifts.  
• Create visual summaries if needed.

• CodeGraph_Agent for statistical calculations and chart scripts.  
• ThinkGraph_Agent for modeling causal or correlational factors.

4. Output  
• Metrics, charts, and explanatory analysis.

5. Trigger to Proceed  
• Quantitative insights clearly answer part of the research question.

6. Decision Point  
• If additional indicators are needed → fetch more data or refine metrics.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 6. Qualitative and Contextual Analysis  
1. Input  
• Quantitative results  
• Policy, regulatory, and industry information

2. Actions  
• Evaluate tariff and non‑tariff barriers.  
• Assess political, supply‑chain, and market environment.  
• Identify competitor positioning.

3. Tools/Agents  
• ThinkGraph_Agent to integrate qualitative and quantitative insights.  
• KnowGraph_Agent to extract structured knowledge from trusted sources.

4. Output  
• Narrative assessment of risks, opportunities, and market dynamics.

5. Trigger to Proceed  
• Contextual factors have been mapped to quantitative results.

6. Decision Point  
• If gaps exist → return to Phases 2–3 for supplemental data.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 7. Synthesis and Reporting  
1. Input  
• Quantitative and qualitative insights

2. Actions  
• Produce cohesive conclusion and recommendations.  
• Prepare summary, charts, and final deliverables.  
• Organize findings by opportunities, risks, partners, and market entry notes.

3. Tools/Agents  
• ThinkGraph_Agent for synthesis logic.

4. Output  
• Final trade‑research report  
• Executive summary  
• Curated dataset or code (optional)

5. Trigger to Proceed  
• User confirms report completeness.

6. Decision Point  
• If the findings need deeper analysis → return to relevant phase.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 8. Optional Automation and Template Reuse  
1. Input  
• Final workflow + scripts

2. Actions  
• Build reusable templates for similar research tasks.  
• Automate data refresh cycles or scheduled updates.

3. Tools/Agents  
• CodeGraph_Agent for automation.

4. Output  
• Reusable pipeline + template for future trade‑research tasks.

• If user wants ongoing monitoring → enable periodic data pulls.

events.outputSummary:
Here’s a complete, direct answer to your original request — proposing the agents/tools you need and giving you a clear plan for using me for trade research.

------------------------------------------------------------
YOUR TRADE‑RESEARCH SETUP
------------------------------------------------------------

To conduct trade research efficiently with me, here are the agents and tools I recommend using, along with what each one does:

AGENTS YOU WILL USE  
• ThinkGraph_Agent  
  Helps map out the trade ecosystem: relationships between products, markets, supply chains, risks, regulations, competitors, etc.  
  Best for conceptual reasoning and structuring complex problems.

• KnowGraph_Agent  
  Identifies, organizes, and validates external data sources such as:  
  – UN Comtrade  
  – WTO datasets  
  – World Bank indicators  
  – Tariff databases  
  – National trade statistics  
  Best for building a structured knowledge map of where to get reliable data.

• CodeGraph_Agent  
  Retrieves, cleans, and processes trade datasets.  
  Can run API queries (e.g., UN Comtrade API), clean data, calculate indicators, and generate charts.  
  Best for automation, data handling, and analytics.

• Plan_Agent  
  Builds step‑by‑step workflows and ensures your trade‑research process is structured, complete, and repeatable.  
  Best for organizing the entire research operation.

------------------------------------------------------------
YOUR TRADE‑RESEARCH PLAN  
------------------------------------------------------------

Here is the operational plan you can follow for any sector or HS code:

1. Define Scope  
Tell me what sector, product, or HS code you want to study.  
ThinkGraph_Agent maps the problem and clarifies what data we need.

2. Map Data Sources  
KnowGraph_Agent lists the best sources for trade flows, tariffs, supply‑chain data, industry reports, and regulations.

3. Acquire Data  
CodeGraph_Agent retrieves and downloads data (e.g., from UN Comtrade, WTO, etc.).  
If needed, it automates API queries and bulk downloads.

4. Clean and Structure Data  
CodeGraph_Agent formats, normalizes, and structures the data so it’s ready for analysis.  
ThinkGraph_Agent helps design how the data should be organized.

5. Quantitative Analysis  
CodeGraph_Agent runs growth calculations, partner analysis, trade balances, price trends, and visual summaries.  
ThinkGraph_Agent models causal and structural relationships.

6. Qualitative Analysis  
ThinkGraph_Agent integrates the quantitative data with regulatory, geopolitical, and industry‑level insights.  
KnowGraph_Agent finds relevant high‑quality information to support analysis.

7. Synthesis and Reporting  
I produce a complete trade‑research report including:  
• Opportunities  
• Risks  
• Competitors  
• Trends  
• Partner analysis  
• Market‑entry or sourcing recommendations

8. Automation (Optional)  
If you want ongoing monitoring, CodeGraph_Agent can set up automated data refreshes and recreate the analysis on a schedule.

------------------------------------------------------------
NEXT STEP FOR YOU  
Just tell me:  
• the product or sector, OR  
• the HS codes you want to research.  
I will launch the workflow immediately.

Below is a clean, reusable, end‑to‑end workflow for conducting trade research. It is modular and identifies where each agent (ThinkGraph_Agent, KnowGraph_Agent, CodeGraph_Agent) is most useful.

------------------------------------------------------------
END‑TO‑END TRADE‑RESEARCH WORKFLOW
------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 1: Define Scope and Research Question  
Tasks:  
• Identify the target sector, products, HS codes, or markets.  
• Specify geographic focus (countries, regions).  
• Establish what the research aims to answer (e.g., opportunities, risks, competitive landscape).  

Agents/Tools:  
• ThinkGraph_Agent: Map the conceptual domain, variables, stakeholders, and interdependencies.  

Deliverables:  
• Clear research brief.  
• Concept map of relevant factors.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 2: Identify and Validate Data Sources  
Tasks:  
• List relevant trade‑data repositories (UN Comtrade, WTO, World Bank, national customs databases).  
• Identify tariff, regulatory, and logistics data sources.  
• Identify industry‑specific sources (Statista, OECD, sector reports).  
• Validate availability, access method, freshness, and licensing.  

Agents/Tools:  
• KnowGraph_Agent: Build a structured map of all data sources and their attributes.  

Deliverables:  
• Validated data‑source map.  
• Reference list for automated or manual retrieval.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 3: Data Acquisition  
Tasks:  
• Retrieve trade‑flow data (imports, exports, partners, volumes, values).  
• Retrieve tariffs, duties, and non‑tariff barrier information.  
• Retrieve supply‑chain or logistics‑cost data if applicable.  
• Download metadata (units, methodologies).  

Agents/Tools:  
• CodeGraph_Agent:  
  - Write scripts or API queries (e.g., UN Comtrade API).  
  - Automate bulk download and data cleaning.  
  - Standardize formats (CSV, JSON, database tables).  

Deliverables:  
• Clean, unified database of relevant trade data.  
• Scripts for automated updates.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 4: Data Structuring and Pre‑Processing  
Tasks:  
• Normalize product codes (HS code mapping).  
• Convert currency and units if needed.  
• Handle missing data and outliers.  
• Tag data with metadata (country codes, temporal frequency).  

Agents/Tools:  
• CodeGraph_Agent: Data cleaning, transformation, normalization.  
• ThinkGraph_Agent: Create logical relationships among data components (products, partners, time, indicators).  

Deliverables:  
• Analysis‑ready structured dataset.  
• Schema or data dictionary.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 5: Quantitative Analysis  
Tasks:  
• Compute time‑series trends, growth rates, concentration indices.  
• Identify major trading partners and share splits.  
• Analyze competitiveness, trade balance, or market dynamics.  
• Identify anomalies or structural breaks.  

Agents/Tools:  
• CodeGraph_Agent: Implement statistical calculations, visualizations, or modeling.  
• ThinkGraph_Agent: Conceptual modeling of causal or correlational factors.  

Deliverables:  
• Analytical metrics and insights.  
• Charts or summarized outputs.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 6: Qualitative and Contextual Analysis  
Tasks:  
• Review relevant policies, regulations, and geopolitical factors.  
• Assess supply‑chain structures, logistics constraints, or market maturity.  
• Evaluate competitor landscape.  

Agents/Tools:  
• ThinkGraph_Agent: Integrate qualitative and quantitative factors into a coherent conceptual view.  
• KnowGraph_Agent: Supplement contextual information from structured knowledge sources.  

Deliverables:  
• Narrative analysis.  
• Risk and opportunity framework.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 7: Synthesis and Reporting  
Tasks:  
• Consolidate quantitative and qualitative findings.  
• Summarize opportunities, risks, and strategic recommendations.  
• Produce sector‑specific or market‑specific insights.  
• Package outputs for presentation.

Agents/Tools:  
• ThinkGraph_Agent: Create final reasoning maps and synthesis views.  

Deliverables:  
• Final trade‑research report.  
• Executive summary.  
• Supporting charts and datasets.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 8: Optional Automation and Reuse  
Tasks:  
• Convert workflow into reusable templates for new sectors or countries.  
• Automate data refresh cycles.  

Agents/Tools:  
• CodeGraph_Agent: Build pipelines for automated or periodic updates.  
• KnowGraph_Agent: Maintain updated data‑source catalogs.  

Deliverables:  
• Modular research template.  
• Automated update scripts or workflows.

------------------------------------------------------------

If you want, I can also assemble these steps into a structured plan card for approval, or generate a modular template you can reuse across multiple trade sectors.

Here is a clean, operational, step‑by‑step plan you can run immediately once you specify the trade sector or HS codes. It includes inputs, outputs, triggers, and decision points. It is modular and reusable.

------------------------------------------------------------
OPERATIONAL TRADE‑RESEARCH PLAN  
(Ready for immediate execution once sector/HS codes are provided)
------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 1. Scope Definition  
1. Input  
• User‑specified sector, product, or HS codes  
• Target countries or regions (optional)  
• Research objective (opportunities, risks, competitors, trends)

2. Actions  
• Clarify research question.  
• Identify required indicators (trade flows, tariffs, partners, market size).

3. Tools/Agents  
• ThinkGraph_Agent to map problem space.

4. Output  
• Defined research brief and concept map.

5. Trigger to Proceed  
• Research question is specific enough to search for data.

6. Decision Point  
• If HS codes are unknown → trigger “HS code identification” subroutine using KnowGraph_Agent.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 2. Data‑Source Mapping  
1. Input  
• Research brief  
• Required indicators

2. Actions  
• Identify which repositories hold the needed data (trade flows, tariffs, regulations).  
• Validate API access, rate limits, and data availability.

3. Tools/Agents  
• KnowGraph_Agent to build a structured source map.

4. Output  
• Prioritized list of data sources with retrieval instructions.

5. Trigger to Proceed  
• All required data sources identified and accessible.

6. Decision Point  
• If a critical source is unavailable → choose a substitute or revise scope.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 3. Data Acquisition  
1. Input  
• Source map with APIs, endpoints, and datasets  
• HS codes and countries

2. Actions  
• Query trade‑flow databases (UN Comtrade, WTO, national stats).  
• Download tariffs and regulatory data.  
• Pull logistics/supply‑chain data when relevant.

3. Tools/Agents  
• CodeGraph_Agent to write API calls, handle pagination, and download files.

4. Output  
• Raw datasets stored locally or in a workspace.

5. Trigger to Proceed  
• All essential data retrieved without errors.

6. Decision Point  
• If any dataset is incomplete or missing → retry retrieval or switch to alternate sources.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 4. Data Cleaning and Structuring  
1. Input  
• Raw datasets  
• Metadata and codebooks

2. Actions  
• Clean missing values, normalize units, standardize country and HS codes.  
• Create standardized tables (imports, exports, partners, time series).  
• Document transformations.

3. Tools/Agents  
• CodeGraph_Agent for cleaning scripts.  
• ThinkGraph_Agent to design data schema.

4. Output  
• Analysis‑ready dataset with metadata.

5. Trigger to Proceed  
• Data passes basic quality checks.

6. Decision Point  
• If data quality is poor → return to Phase 3 for better sources.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 5. Quantitative Analysis  
1. Input  
• Structured dataset  
• Research questions

2. Actions  
• Compute growth trends, partner concentrations, trade balance, price trends.  
• Identify anomalies or structural shifts.  
• Create visual summaries if needed.

3. Tools/Agents  
• CodeGraph_Agent for statistical calculations and chart scripts.  
• ThinkGraph_Agent for modeling causal or correlational factors.

4. Output  
• Metrics, charts, and explanatory analysis.

5. Trigger to Proceed  
• Quantitative insights clearly answer part of the research question.

6. Decision Point  
• If additional indicators are needed → fetch more data or refine metrics.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 6. Qualitative and Contextual Analysis  
1. Input  
• Quantitative results  
• Policy, regulatory, and industry information

2. Actions  
• Evaluate tariff and non‑tariff barriers.  
• Assess political, supply‑chain, and market environment.  
• Identify competitor positioning.

3. Tools/Agents  
• ThinkGraph_Agent to integrate qualitative and quantitative insights.  
• KnowGraph_Agent to extract structured knowledge from trusted sources.

4. Output  
• Narrative assessment of risks, opportunities, and market dynamics.

5. Trigger to Proceed  
• Contextual factors have been mapped to quantitative results.

6. Decision Point  
• If gaps exist → return to Phases 2–3 for supplemental data.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 7. Synthesis and Reporting  
1. Input  
• Quantitative and qualitative insights

2. Actions  
• Produce cohesive conclusion and recommendations.  
• Prepare summary, charts, and final deliverables.  
• Organize findings by opportunities, risks, partners, and market entry notes.

3. Tools/Agents  
• ThinkGraph_Agent for synthesis logic.

4. Output  
• Final trade‑research report  
• Executive summary  
• Curated dataset or code (optional)

5. Trigger to Proceed  
• User confirms report completeness.

6. Decision Point  
• If the findings need deeper analysis → return to relevant phase.

------------------------------------------------------------

PHASE 8. Optional Automation and Template Reuse  
1. Input  
• Final workflow + scripts

2. Actions  
• Build reusable templates for similar research tasks.  
• Automate data refresh cycles or scheduled updates.

3. Tools/Agents  
• CodeGraph_Agent for automation.

4. Output  
• Reusable pipeline + template for future trade‑research tasks.

5. Decision Point  
• If user wants ongoing monitoring → enable periodic data pulls.

------------------------------------------------------------

Here’s a complete, direct answer to your original request — proposing the agents/tools you need and giving you a clear plan for using me for trade research.

------------------------------------------------------------
YOUR TRADE‑RESEARCH SETUP
------------------------------------------------------------

To conduct trade research efficiently with me, here are the agents and tools I recommend using, along with what each one does:

AGENTS YOU WILL USE  
• ThinkGraph_Agent  
  Helps map out the trade ecosystem: relationships between products, markets, supply chains, risks, regulations, competitors, etc.  
  Best for conceptual reasoning and structuring complex problems.

• KnowGraph_Agent  
  Identifies, organizes, and validates external data sources such as:  
  – UN Comtrade  
  – WTO datasets  
  – World Bank indicators  
  – Tariff databases  
  – National trade statistics  
  Best for building a structured knowledge map of where to get reliable data.

• CodeGraph_Agent  
  Retrieves, cleans, and processes trade datasets.  
  Can run API queries (e.g., UN Comtrade API), clean data, calculate indicators, and generate charts.  
  Best for automation, data handling, and analytics.

• Plan_Agent  
  Builds step‑by‑step workflows and ensures your trade‑research process is structured, complete, and repeatable.  
  Best for organizing the entire research operation.

------------------------------------------------------------
YOUR TRADE‑RESEARCH PLAN  
------------------------------------------------------------

Here is the operational plan you can follow for any sector or HS code:

1. Define Scope  
Tell me what sector, product, or HS code you want to study.  
ThinkGraph_Agent maps the problem and clarifies what data we need.

2. Map Data Sources  
KnowGraph_Agent lists the best sources for trade flows, tariffs, supply‑chain data, industry reports, and regulations.

3. Acquire Data  
CodeGraph_Agent retrieves and downloads data (e.g., from UN Comtrade, WTO, etc.).  
If needed, it automates API queries and bulk downloads.

4. Clean and Structure Data  
CodeGraph_Agent formats, normalizes, and structures the data so it’s ready for analysis.  
ThinkGraph_Agent helps design how the data should be organized.

5. Quantitative Analysis  
CodeGraph_Agent runs growth calculations, partner analysis, trade balances, price trends, and visual summaries.  
ThinkGraph_Agent models causal and structural relationships.

6. Qualitative Analysis  
ThinkGraph_Agent integrates the quantitative data with regulatory, geopolitical, and industry‑level insights.  
KnowGraph_Agent finds relevant high‑quality information to support analysis.

7. Synthesis and Reporting  
I produce a complete trade‑research report including:  
• Opportunities  
• Risks  
• Competitors  
• Trends  
• Partner analysis  
• Market‑entry or sourcing recommendations

8. Automation (Optional)  
If you want ongoing monitoring, CodeGraph_Agent can set up automated data refreshes and recreate the analysis on a schedule.

------------------------------------------------------------
NEXT STEP FOR YOU  
Just tell me:  
• the product or sector, OR  
• the HS codes you want to research.  
I will launch the workflow immediately.
